title: BodyType uses Unknown(String) not serde(other) because it is serialized as an outbound field
description: BodyType is used for both inbound deserialization (Message.body_type from server) and outbound serialization (MessageCreateInput.body_type, MessagePatch.body_type sent to server). A unit Unknown variant via serde(other) would silently discard the original wire string on deserialization, making it impossible to echo a non-standard MIME type back in a patch or create call. The hand-rolled Serialize/Deserialize is intentional: it maps text/plain and text/markdown to typed variants and stores any other string in Unknown(String) for lossless preservation. Do not replace with serde(other) to match the other catch-all enums. Those are deserialization-only objects (DeliveryState, ChatKind, ChatMemberRole, etc.), whereas BodyType must survive a round-trip through both directions of the wire.
